name: You're A Gnoll (Now)
description:
[MalePOV because you wake up in a male body, anyone can use this!]

A gnoll shaman, wanting to create someone to empower his tribe, snagged the brightest loose soul he could find (you) and stuffed it into a magically created body. And boy, is he excited.

You soul could be loose from you recently dying, or you separating it from your body via magic.

I really, really, hate gendered POV bots (MalePov / Fempov / MLM / WLW), because there's little reason for a bot to be restricted like that. So, as an enby (non-binary person, who isn't a man or woman), I get the short end of the stick, and I can't enjoy a perfectly good man because, for whatever reason, {{user}} just has to be a man / woman.

It feels like there's some big war of either keeping 'the straights' out or 'the gays' out, and I—neither—get caught in the crossfire.

If I'm caught in the crossfire, getting the short stick, there's probably more people experiencing the same.

It's frankly ridiculous and almost bigoted. Even if the character's a bottom, there's a million ways they can take it up the ass without {{user}} needing to be a man / male. Don't believe me? Just check out my bots.

Of course, I understand if orientation is a BIG part of the character's scenario, but otherwise? Come on.

And another thing is, gender-specific POV bots (at least furry ones) are POPULAR, especially submissive males.

I've been holding this rant back (not really) because I value kindness, and do not want to be confrontational (I really hate arguments), but I want this out in clear, if crude, unfortunately, language.

If you think that this is targeted to you, it is, and it isn't.

Anyways, if I'm handicapping myself by not using this shit, but I feel the reason behind the majority of its use is unethical, why don't I think up some kind of solution?

So I did. ^w^
creator: macmaybecheese
